title: Who will manage to capture the 'middle' electorate Macron seized 10 years ago?
subhed: Among French President Emmanuel Macron's political heirs, and even on the left, some are starting to believe that the presidential campaign could play out in the middle, amid the collapse of France's former governing parties.

title: 38° at 11 am. French students and staff struggle with unbearable classroom temperatures

The resilience of French growth appears to be reaching its limits
subhed: France's gross domestic product for the first quarter of 2026 has been revised down to -0.1%. While the government blames 'wait-and-see' behavior, several indicators suggest a worsening economic climate.

title: Why Trump lashed out at Oman over Strait of Hormuz blockade
subhed: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday that Washington 'will not tolerate any effort ​to impose a tolling ​system' with Iran in the strait, confirming the president's displeasure expressed the previous day.

title: WWF France president resigns after attendance at anti-racism protest sparks controversy
subhed: Alexandra Palt was criticized by the board of the World Wildlife Fund for France for mentioning on LinkedIn that she had attended an April rally organized by Bally Bagayoko, the mayor of Saint-Denis and a member of the hard-left La France Insoumise party.

title: 'Paris must remain a city that knows how to celebrate major events'
subhed: In response to the Paris Police Prefecture's announcement that there will be no parade on the Champs-Elysées if PSG wins the Champions League final on Saturday, essayist Adrien Motel says he regrets that the French capital too often views celebrations and as a potential threat.
